title: _PyObject_GC_UNTRACK assert in dictiter_dealloc (Objects/dictobject.c) when dictiter_new() decref's an untracked item-iterator under MemoryError
description: `Objects/dictobject.c`: `dictiter_new()` Py_DECREF's a not-yet-GC-tracked `dict_itemiterator` when `_PyTuple_FromPairSteal(di_result)` fails under OOM; `dictiter_dealloc` then `_PyObject_GC_UNTRACK`s an untracked object
notes: Assertion bug: fires only on assertion-enabled builds (ft_debug_asan abort, jit abort). ft_release=n/a (NDEBUG strips assert; no segv on that run). upstream (release+ASan) segv in _Py_Dealloc -- same root, NDEBUG corrupts GC list instead of asserting; this is why the zipapp vehicle was labeled 'segfault' yet trips the identical dictiter assert on the debug build (verified). Only item iterators affected (dict_itemiterator) -- only kind whose di_result needs an alloc between PyObject_GC_New and _PyObject_GC_TRACK. MINIMAL REPRO (shrinkray-reduced from the vehicle, then cleaned; deterministic, re-verified): `_strptime._strptime("", "")` under the set_nomemory sweep.
